CHUMMER.PdfConfigHint: Map each sourcebook code (SR5, RG, …) to a PDF file and page offset.
CHUMMER.OffsetHint: Offset = PDF page − printed page number
CHUMMER.BookConfigHint: Choose which sourcebooks character creation, shop, quick NPC and karma advancement offer entries from. The core rulebook is always active.
CHUMMER.PdfOpenError: No PDF file configured for sourcebook "{code}".
CHUMMER.PdfDefaultsApplied: PDF sources configured automatically ({count} books from the pdfs/ folder).
CHUMMER.PacksTranslated: Compendiums translated: {renamed} names, {sources} source references, {folders} folders.

PriorityDesc.Heritage: Determines the selectable metatypes and the special points for Edge, Magic, or Resonance.
PriorityDesc.Attributes: Points for the eight core attributes (Body through Willpower).
PriorityDesc.Talent: Whether and how strongly the character has Magic or Resonance – E means mundane.
PriorityDesc.Skills: Points for skills and skill groups.
PriorityDesc.Resources: Starting funds for gear, cyberware, and lifestyle.

Intro.Priorities: Name the character, pick the character type, and assign priorities A (strong) to E (weak) – each letter exactly once. Priorities set all budgets for the following steps. Beginner tip: prioritize attributes highly, and pick Magic/Resonance only if the character should cast spells or thread the Matrix (otherwise E).
Intro.Metatype: Choose the character's people. The cards show attribute ranges (green = better than human, red = worse) and the special points ✦ that later go into Edge, Magic, or Resonance. Dashed cards are metavariants.
Intro.Attributes: Distribute attribute points between the metatype's minimum and maximum. Special points ✦ are spent separately on Edge, Magic, or Resonance. Both budgets should be fully spent in the end.
Intro.Qualities: Optional: positive qualities cost karma, negative qualities grant karma – at most 25 each. Use the source reference (e.g. SR5 74) to look up the rule directly in the PDF.
Intro.Skills: Skill groups raise all contained skills at once and can only be paid with group points. Individual skills (rating 1–6) use the regular skill points.
Intro.Talent: Choose the specific form of magic or resonance, then pick spells, adept powers, or complex forms. Free skills from the talent don't cost skill points.
Intro.Gear: Buy starting gear from your starting funds. Availability above the limit or “F” (forbidden) is normally not allowed during creation. Up to 10 karma can be converted into 2,000¥ each.
Intro.Summary: Review the open points, then create the character. All values can be adjusted later on the character sheet – purchases and improvements continue via Shop and Karma Advancement.
Intro.Background: Contacts (Charisma × 3 points for connection and loyalty) and knowledge/language skills ((INT + LOG) × 2 points). Your native language is free.

ActorType.Label: Character type
ActorType.pcDesc: Full character for a player. The token is linked to the character sheet (friendly).
ActorType.npcDesc: Non-player character for the gamemaster – independent tokens, neutral disposition.
ActorType.gruntDesc: NPC group member with a shared condition monitor and hostile disposition – ideal for opposition squads.

CHUMMER.PriorityDuplicate: Each priority (A–E) may only be assigned once.
CHUMMER.NameMissing: The character still needs a name.
CHUMMER.NoHeritagePrio: First assign a priority to Metatype in the “Priorities” step – it determines which peoples are available.
CHUMMER.NoMetatype: First choose a metatype – it sets the attribute ranges.
CHUMMER.NoTalentPrio: First assign a priority to Magic/Resonance in the “Priorities” step.
CHUMMER.MundaneNote: This character is mundane – there is nothing more to pick here. Continue with Gear.
CHUMMER.MetatypeHint: Attribute ranges marked green exceed the human maximum of 6, red ones fall below it. ✦ = special points for Edge/Magic/Resonance.
CHUMMER.GroupHint: Groups raise all contained skills at once – payable only with group points.
CHUMMER.AllDone: All done – the character can be created.
CHUMMER.FreeSkillHint: Included in the talent and free of charge – they don't use skill points.
CHUMMER.Name: Name
CHUMMER.SpecialHint: Special points come from the metatype priority and go into Edge, Magic, or Resonance.
CHUMMER.KarmaToNuyen: Convert karma to nuyen (2,000¥ per karma, max. {max})
CHUMMER.AvailWarning: Availability above {limit} – normally not allowed during creation.

Settings.AvailLimitHint: Maximum availability for gear during character creation (default: 12).
Settings.ChargenKarmaHint: Karma budget at the start of character creation (default: 25).
Settings.UseCompendiumHint: When buying, first look up the full item in the compendiums created by the Chummer bulk importer (sr5weapon, sr5gear, …).

CHUMMER.MissingCompendiumHint: Tip: Run the system's Chummer data import once (Settings → Import Chummer Data) so purchased items are created with full game stats.

QuickNpc.Title: Quick NPC (CRB templates)
QuickNpc.Intro: Click a template and customize it: attributes, skills, spells/powers, and gear can be changed freely before saving. From two copies on, the squad is numbered and placed in its own folder. For rules-legal characters, use “Create Character (Chummer)”.
QuickNpc.EditHint: All values can be changed freely – NPC stats don't have to follow creation rules.
QuickNpc.GruntToggle: Mark as grunt: shared condition monitor and group edge equal to the professional rating.

Grunts.Title: Import Grunts
Grunts.ReplaceHint: The grunt compendium already exists. Delete and recreate it? Any custom changes to the grunts will be lost.
Grunts.Done: {count} grunts have been imported into the “SR5 Grunts (CRB)” compendium.
Grunts.ProfRating: Professional Rating {pr} – group edge {pr} (the edge attribute equals the group edge).

Enrich.GmOnly: Only the GM can enrich existing items.
Enrich.Summary: Core-book enrichment: {matched} items recognized, {descriptions} descriptions added, {effects} effects created ({scanned} scanned).
Enrich.MenuHint: Adds core-book descriptions and effects to all existing world and character items (details in the console log, F12).

Repair.Summary: Data repair: fixed {count} invalid effect tint values (details in console).

Import.Title: Chummer Import
Import.Intro: Load a JSON export from the Chummer5a desktop app and create a new character or sync it into an existing one. Items are matched against the core rulebook catalog and enriched automatically.
Import.SyncHint: Re-sync: existing items are kept (ratings updated), new ones are added. Damage, edge and ammo are never touched.
Import.OptOrphans: Remove imported items no longer present
Import.ResultSkills: Skills: {updated} ratings set, {created} created, {deduped} duplicates removed.

CHUMMER.ContactHint: Connection + loyalty per contact count against the budget (Charisma × 3).
CHUMMER.KnowledgeHint: Knowledge and language skills, budget (Intuition + Logic) × 2.
CHUMMER.TraditionHint: The tradition determines the second drain attribute (besides Willpower).
